channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Federal Budget 2026 Introduces Tax, Trust, and Regulatory Reforms Amid Uncertain Economy
item.description: The 2026 Federal Budget outlines a mixture of tax, spending, and regulatory reforms designed to improve economic stability and productivity amid ongoing uncertainty. The budget deficit is projected at...

item.title: GEPF Reports R200 Billion Loss Amid Middle East Tensions, Plans Local Investments
item.description: The Government Employees Pension Fund (GEPF), which manages the retirement savings of over 1.7 million public servants and pensioners, suffered a substantial loss of R200 billion during the first week...

item.title: Court of Appeal Allows Kenya-US Health Partnership to Proceed Pending Final Ruling
item.description: The Court of Appeal in Nairobi has temporarily lifted the High Court’s orders that had previously blocked the implementation of a Kenya-US health cooperation programme. This decision allows the govern...

item.title: Charles Verschuuren Jr., Dutch-American Illustrator and Portrait Painter
item.description: Charles Verschuuren Jr. (1891–1955) was a talented Dutch-American illustrator and painter who successfully navigated both commercial art and fine portraiture. Born in the Netherlands into an artistic ...

item.title: Students report possible leak of AS-Level Mathematics paper; Cambridge International responds
item.description: Students appearing for the Cambridge AS-Level Mathematics examination in Pakistan have raised concerns that the paper they received on Tuesday may have been leaked prior to the exam. According to thei...

item.title: Stock Market Decline Causes Huge Losses, NEET UG Exam Cancelled Amid Paper Leak
item.description: On Tuesday, the Indian stock market suffered a significant setback, with the Sensex dropping over 1,450 points and the Nifty falling by more than 430 points, marking a decline of around 1.8-1.9%. This...

item.title: VP Sara Duterte Lets Lawyers Handle Impeachment Proceedings, Remains Neutral
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte addressed the recent impeachment proceedings against her in an interview in The Hague, stating that she is leaving the matter to her lawyers and spokesperson. Duterte empha...

item.title: Study Finds Half of Caribbean Reefs with Highest Coastal Protection Value Remain Unprotected
item.description: Coral reefs serve as vital natural barriers against hurricanes and storm surges in the Caribbean, reducing wave energy by up to 97% and preventing billions of dollars in annual storm damage globally. ...

item.title: EFCC Operatives Visit Uyo Teaching Hospital for Investigation, Allege Attack and Obstruction by Staff
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) on Tuesday allege say im operatives wey go University of Uyo Teaching Hospital (UUTH) to verify one medical report for one fraud suspect bin face at...

item.title: Jane Fonda and Gong Li inaugurate the 79th Cannes Film Festival
item.description: American film legend Jane Fonda and renowned Chinese actress Gong Li officially opened the 79th Cannes Film Festival on the French Riviera. The ceremony, attended by a glittering array of internationa...

item.title: NASA Landsat Interactive Uses Satellite Imagery to Spell Names with Earth’s Landscapes
item.description: NASA, in collaboration with the U.S. Geological Survey, has introduced a creative Earth Day 2026 project called “Your Name in Landsat,” which transforms satellite imagery into a playful, personalized ...

item.title: Himanta Biswa Sarma and Suvendu Adhikari Share Friendly Moment at Assam Government Swearing-In
item.description: During the swearing-in ceremony of the Assam government, Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma and West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari were seen sharing a warm moment, attracting public att...

item.title: AS Level Mathematics second paper leaked before exam, IBCC and Cambridge respond
item.description: The second paper of AS-level Mathematics under Cambridge International Education was reportedly leaked on social media before the scheduled examination time on Tuesday. Students informed Geo News that...

item.title: Supporting New Nurse Graduates in Critical Care Units is Essential for Patient Safety
item.description: On International Nurses Day, Isabella du Preez highlights the pressing need to support new nurse graduates (NNGs) entering critical care units (CCUs) in South Africa. Despite being qualified as genera...

item.title: Experts urge unified tax system to strengthen Nigeria’s economy
item.description: Stakeholders don yan say make Nigeria get one unified and harmonised tax framework as part of the ongoing fiscal reforms wey dey try make government revenue strong, make compliance better, and drive l...

item.title: Safa Secures R20 Million to Introduce VAR in South African Professional Football
item.description: South African football is set to finally see the rollout of Video Assistant Referee (VAR) technology, with the South African Football Association (Safa) receiving R20 million from the government to im...

item.title: Affordable Nail Strengthener Review: Modelones Milky Nail Concealer for Brittle Nails
item.description: If you struggle with thin, brittle nails or damage from acrylics and gel manicures, the Modelones Milky Nail Concealer may be a budget-friendly solution. Priced under $10 on Amazon, this 3-in-1 produc...

item.title: Donald Trump to meet Xi Jinping in Beijing amid trade, Middle East, and tech discussions
item.description: United States President Donald Trump is scheduled to visit Beijing from 14–15 May for a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping, marking the first such meeting on Chinese soil in nearly a dec...

item.title: Survivors of Jeffrey Epstein testify near Mar-a-Lago, calling for accountability from authorities
item.description: Survivors of Jeffrey Epstein’s abuse gathered in West Palm Beach, near President Trump’s Mar-a-Lago estate, to give testimony demanding accountability from the Department of Justice. The hearing, orga...

item.title: City of Joburg enforces bylaws by removing illegal structures in CBD
item.description: Residents of Johannesburg are split over the recent clean-up operations in the city’s central business district, where the local authorities have been demolishing illegally built structures and removi...
